heading: Cause of action for misrepresentation of Indian produced goods
label: 305e Cause of action for misrepresentation of Indian produced goods
notes: References in TextThis Act, referred to in subsec. (f), is , , which is classified generally to section 305 et seq. of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.act Aug. 27, 1935, ch. 74849 Stat. 891

children.label: A
children.text: is a member of an Indian tribe; or

children.label: B
children.text: is certified as an Indian artisan by an Indian tribe.

children.heading: Indian product
children.label: 2 Indian product
children.text: The term “Indian product” has the meaning given the term in any regulation promulgated by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a State legislature;

children.label: ii
children.text: a State commission; or

children.label: iii
children.text: another similar organization vested with State legislative tribal recognition authority.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 4 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Injunctive or equitable relief; damages
children.label: b Injunctive or equitable relief; damages

children.text: obtain injunctive or other equitable relief; and

children.label: A
children.text: treble damages; or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of each aggrieved individual Indian, Indian tribe, or Indian arts and crafts organization, not less than $1,000 for each day on which the offer or display for sale or sale continues.

children.heading: Punitive damages; attorney’s fee
children.label: c Punitive damages; attorney’s fee
children.text: In addition to the relief specified in subsection (b), the court may award punitive damages and the costs of the civil action and a reasonable attorney’s fee.

children.heading: Persons that may initiate civil actions
children.label: d Persons that may initiate civil actions
i
children.text: an Indian tribe;

children.label: ii
children.text: an Indian; or

children.label: iii
children.text: an Indian arts and crafts organization;

children.label: i
children.text: the Indian tribe;

children.label: ii
children.text: a member of that Indian tribe; or

children.label: iii
children.text: an Indian arts and crafts organization;

children.label: C
children.text: an Indian; or

children.label: D
children.text: an Indian arts and crafts organization.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), an amount recovered in a civil action under this section shall be paid to the Indian tribe, the Indian, or the Indian arts and crafts organization on the behalf of which the civil action was initiated.

children.label: I
children.text: the amount of the cost of the civil action and reasonable attorney’s fees awarded under subsection (c), to be deposited in the Treasury and credited to appropriations available to the Attorney General on the date on which the amount is recovered; and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of the costs of investigation awarded under subsection (c), to reimburse the Board for the activities of the Board relating to the civil action.

children.label: I
children.text: the amount of the cost of the civil action; and

children.label: II
children.text: reasonable attorney’s fees.

children.heading: Savings provision
children.label: e Savings provision
children.text: If any provision of this section is held invalid, it is the intent of Congress that the remaining provisions of this section shall continue in full force and effect.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: f Regulations
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Board shall promulgate regulations to include in the definition of the term “Indian product” specific examples of such product to provide guidance to Indian artisans as well as to purveyors and consumers of Indian arts and crafts, as defined under this Act.November 9, 2000
